data.title: Hyperliquid Slips Below $60 As Multicoin, Paradigm Unstake $291M In HYPE
data.description: Hyperliquid’s HYPE fell below $60 after two major funds unstaked about $291 million of the token in days, stoking fears of selling. Multicoin said it is not selling and on-chain data showed the tokens moving to custody rather than to market, according to BeInCrypto, while Paradigm has not commented. HYPE was last trading at $58, about 24% below its June record of $76.70.

data.title: Clarity Act expected to miss its window before Congress' summer break
data.description: The crypto market structure bill known as the Clarity Act is likely to miss the deadline to clear the Senate before lawmakers leave for their summer break, Majority Leader Thune said. The bill may still get a start before then, according to CoinDesk, but leadership signaled the full effort will not be completed in time.

data.title: Iraq's Prime Minister: Iraq Will Not Allow Actions Threatening Iran to Be Launched From Its Territory
data.description: According to Jin10, Iraq's Prime Minister Zaidi visited Iran on July 23 and held talks with Iranian President Pezeshkian in Tehran, Iran's capital, in a statement issued by the Iraqi Prime Minister's office on July 23. Zaidi said Iraq and Iran's security are closely linked, and Iraq will never allow any action threatening Iran to be launched from its territory. Pezeshkian said security and stability are important to the development of bilateral relations.

data.title: Zhibao Technology Signs PIPE Term Sheet Involving Roughly 3,500 Bitcoin
data.description:
Zhibao Technology signed a non-binding term sheet for a proposed PIPE financing involving roughly 3,500 Bitcoin, according to NS3.AI. Joyertech and Information OPC would pay for the stock in Bitcoin and designate a majority of Zhibao's board at closing.

The company also received a Nasdaq deficiency letter on July 15 after trading below $1 per share.

data.title: SAP Cloud Revenue Beats Estimates as Legacy Support Cutoff Nears
data.description: SAP SE said cloud revenue grew faster than analysts expected as customers shifted away from on-premises licenses ahead of the company’s deadline to end support for legacy software, according to Bloomberg. The result suggests the migration away from older products is accelerating as the cutoff approaches.

data.title: Intel Forecast Tops Estimates on Data Center Growth
data.description:
Intel Corp. issued a stronger-than-expected revenue forecast for the current period, signaling that rising data center spending is helping drive its turnaround, according to Bloomberg.

The chipmaker said the outlook reflects continued demand tied to data center growth.

data.title: Digital-Asset Finance Moves Beyond Experimental Phase as Wall Street Deepens Crypto Ties
data.description:
Digital-asset finance is moving beyond the industry’s experimental phase as Wall Street’s largest trading firms deepen their ties with crypto companies. According to Bloomberg, the move comes as major trading firms increase their connections with the sector.

The article says the development reflects growing ties between large Wall Street trading firms and crypto companies.

data.title: STOCKS | Intel Rises More Than 9% After Hours on Stronger-Than-Expected Third-Quarter Revenue Outlook
data.description: Intel (INTC.O) rose more than 9% in after-hours U.S. trading. According to Jin10, its third-quarter revenue outlook was higher than expected.

data.title: Iranian Media Says U.S. Air Base in Kuwait Hit by Missiles and Drones
data.description: According to Jin10, Fars News said the U.S. Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait was attacked by missiles and drones, and Kuwait's air defense system was intercepting drones and missiles coming from Iran.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Says GCC Members Should Respond to Participation in Military Action Against Iran
data.description: According to Jin10, Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson said Gulf Cooperation Council member states should respond to participation in military action against Iran.

data.title: OPEC+ May Raise Output Quotas by 188,000 Barrels a Day
data.description: According to three sources, OPEC+ is likely to agree at its August 2 meeting to further raise its production target from September, despite the Iran war again making it difficult for some members to increase output. According to Jin10, the sources said OPEC+ is likely to raise its September production target by about 188,000 barrels a day, the same increase as in June, July, and August. OPEC+ output remains well below quotas because the Iran war has disrupted exports from Gulf members. Data showed OPEC+ production was 36.28 million barrels a day in June, down from nearly 43 million barrels a day in February before the war broke out. Reuters calculations showed that, taking into account the impact of the UAE's exit, another increase of about 188,000 barrels a day in September would fully unwind the 2023 supply cuts.

data.title: Justice Department Withdraws Subpoenas For New York Times Reporters In Trump Air Force One Probe
data.description: According to CNBC, the Department of Justice is withdrawing subpoenas it obtained for New York Times reporters tied to an article about President Donald Trump's new Air Force One jet, prosecutors told a federal judge in New York on Thursday. The move came during a hearing in U.S. District Court in Manhattan, where Judge Arun Subramanian was weighing the Times' motion to quash the subpoenas against its journalists. The New York Times previously reported that the DOJ had told the newspaper late last week it had issued subpoenas to third-party phone service providers seeking several journalists' phone and text messaging records, and that it had also issued subpoenas on July 10 seeking to compel Times reporters to testify before a federal grand jury.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi Says Some in the U.S. Government Ignore Reality and Focus on the 2028 Election
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi said some people inside the U.S. government ignore reality and focus only on the 2028 election. According to Jin10, he said the reckless aggressive actions they are pushing will only make the U.S. president pay a higher price for the agreement sought.

data.title: STOCKS | Vanguard Says Millennials, Gen Z Investors Are Ditching Bonds
data.description: Vanguard says its average investor is steering clear of fixed income, according to Bloomberg. The comment points to younger generations moving away from bonds, with millennials and Gen Z investors among those shifting their portfolios.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump Expands AI Data Center Pledge to Shift Power Costs
data.description:
US President Donald Trump on Thursday welcomed fresh commitments from power utilities and data center developers to make technology companies pay for the electricity needed to run energy-hungry artificial-intelligence systems, according to Bloomberg.

The pledge expands an administration effort aimed at easing the cost burden tied to AI data centers.

data.title: US Mortgage Rates Reach 6.58%, Highest in Nearly a Year
data.description: US mortgage rates climbed to 6.58%, their highest level in nearly a year. According to NS3.AI, Freddie Mac data showed the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage rate rose from 6.55% a week earlier, while 15-year mortgage rates increased to 5.96% from 5.93%.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi Arrives in Cholpon-Ata, Kyrgyzstan
data.description: According to Jin10, Iran's Foreign Ministry said Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi arrived in Cholpon-Ata, Kyrgyzstan.

data.title: DADDY Memecoin Trades 97% Below All-Time High After Tate Arrests
data.description: Andrew Tate’s DADDY memecoin is trading around $0.008, down 97% from its $0.29 all-time high. According to NS3.AI, the token fell 50% over the past seven days after U.S. Marshals arrested Tate and Tristan Tate on Saturday on a sealed warrant tied to UK extradition proceedings.

data.title: Same Entity Unstakes 2.92 Million HYPE After Eight Months
data.description: Two wallets belonging to the same entity unstaked 2,920,000 HYPE after eight months of staking, according to Onchain Lens. According to Odaily, the two wallets now hold 0 HYPE in the staking contract.

data.title: Ritholtz's Josh Brown says investors should rotate out of Magnificent 7 into broader AI plays
data.description: According to CNBC, Ritholtz Management co-founder and CEO Josh Brown said investors should shift in the second half of this year out of the Magnificent 7 and other hyperscalers and into a wider group of stocks as part of a broadening-out trade. Brown said non-hyperscalers showing business improvement from AI-related upside earnings surprises are the kind of names investors should focus on. He cited Travelers Companies and Chubb Limited as insurers that fit the theme, and he also named Caterpillar and GE Vernova as industrials that offer exposure to the trend. Brown's comments followed Alphabet's Wednesday increase to its 2024 capital expenditures forecast to between $195 billion and $205 billion, a move that triggered selling in hyperscaler stocks on Thursday, including Meta Platforms, Microsoft and Amazon. Travelers and Chubb were both up between 1% and 2% Thursday, while Caterpillar rose about 1% and GE Vernova gained 5%.
